name: pygal (SVG Chart Library for Python)

performance.notes: SVG generation is fast; rendering in browser is lightweight

agent_readiness.notes: Pure SVG chart generation. import pygal. bar_chart = pygal.Bar(). bar_chart.title = 'Browser usage'. bar_chart.add('Firefox', [6.9, 8.3, 7.4, 6.0]). bar_chart.add('Chrome', [52, 49, 51, 55]). bar_chart.render_to_file('chart.svg'). bar_chart.render_to_png('chart.png'). Chart types: Bar, StackedBar, HorizontalBar, Line, StackedLine, Pie, Radar, XY, Dot, Funnel, SolidGauge, Gauge, Box, Treemap. Style: pygal.style.DarkSolarizedStyle. render_response() for Flask/Django HTTP response. Tooltips via SVG title elements. Interactive CSS animations. No JS dependency. render() returns SVG bytes. pygal.maps for country/world maps.

gotchas: LGPL-3.0 — linking requirement for commercial use in compiled products
SVG export quality is high but PNG requires cairosvg (additional dep)
Less rich than plotly or bokeh for interactive features
No type annotations — limited IDE support
Relatively small community and niche use
best_when: SVG chart generation without JavaScript dependencies
Embedding CSS-animated charts in HTML (no JS required)
Django/Flask server-side chart rendering to SVG
Simple charts where plotly/bokeh is too heavy
avoid_when: When plotly or bokeh's interactivity is needed
When PNG is primary output (cairosvg required)
summary: pygal generates SVG charts. Bar/Line/Pie/Radar/XY chart types. .render_to_file('chart.svg'). CSS animations, tooltips. No JS dependency. Flask/Django: render_response(). LGPL-3.0 by Kozea. Best for server-side SVG chart generation without JavaScript. PNG needs cairosvg. Less interactive than plotly. LGPL linking concern for commercial compiled products. Simple API for common chart types.
